description:
You are a database administrator who has been asked to set up a small “query-tuning playground” inside the home directory.  
Your goal is to organise a new project workspace, back-up two sample SQL files that already exist on disk, create “optimised” versions of those queries, and then write a CSV log that will let an automated checker confirm what you have done.

IMPORTANT:  
1.  Every path, filename and extension in this description is case-sensitive; use them exactly as written.  
2.  Do NOT move, rename or delete the original sample files found under “/home/user/sample_queries/”.  
3.  You must create the log file exactly as specified so that the automated tests can parse it.  
4.  Do not use sudo or any root-only locations.  Everything must stay under “/home/user”.  

Step-by-step requirements
-------------------------
1.  Create a new project directory called  
        /home/user/db_opt_project  

    Inside it create exactly three sub-directories:  
        /home/user/db_opt_project/backups  
        /home/user/db_opt_project/queries  
        /home/user/db_opt_project/results  

2.  In “/home/user/sample_queries/” you will already find two files:  
        orders.sql  
        inventory.sql  
    Copy these two files into the new “queries” sub-directory (keep the same filenames).

3.  Make a compressed **tar.gz** archive of everything currently inside “/home/user/db_opt_project/queries”.  
    • Save the archive in “/home/user/db_opt_project/backups”.  
    • The archive MUST be named exactly:  
        queries_backup_20240115.tar.gz  
    After the archive is created, do **not** delete the original files in the “queries” directory.

4.  Create an optimised version of each query:  
    • Write the new files to “/home/user/db_opt_project/results”.  
    • The optimised filenames must be **orders_optimized.sql** and **inventory_optimized.sql**.  
    • Each optimised file must contain (at minimum) the following three lines, in this exact order:  
        Line 1:  a comment header beginning with “-- Optimized” (anything after that text is allowed).  
        Line 2:  a single-line SELECT statement that references the original table, still contains the original filter condition, **and includes the phrase “USE INDEX” somewhere inside the statement**.  
        Line 3:  a comment line that starts with “-- End”.  
      Example layout (the exact SQL text is up to you as long as the above rules are met):  
         -- Optimized version of orders query  
         SELECT col1, col2 FROM orders USE INDEX (some_index) WHERE order_date > '2023-01-01';  
         -- End  

5.  Produce a log file that the automated tests can parse.  
    • Location:   /home/user/db_opt_project/optimization_log.csv  
    • Format: **CSV, no header comment lines, comma-separated values, UTF-8, Unix line endings.**  
    • First row must be a header row spelled exactly:  
            filename,lines_original,lines_optimized  
    • Subsequent rows (one per query) must list:  
        a) the original filename (e.g., “orders.sql”),  
        b) the line count of the original file,  
        c) the line count of its optimised counterpart.  
      Example of the required structure (the numbers are examples; use the real counts you get):  
            filename,lines_original,lines_optimized  
            orders.sql,1,3  
            inventory.sql,1,3  

6.  Double-check that:  
    • All three sub-directories exist.  
    • The backup archive is present with the exact name specified.  
    • Both optimised SQL files exist in “results” and each contains the words “USE INDEX”.  
    • optimization_log.csv is present, has the correct header row, and correct numbers for each file.

If everything is set up correctly, the automated tests will untar the backup, read the SQL files, verify the contents of the optimised files, and parse optimization_log.csv.  Make sure your paths, filenames, and the CSV structure are exactly as described.
truth:
Pre-existing filesystem state (before the agent starts):
1. Directory /home/user/sample_queries/ exists and contains exactly two files:
   a) /home/user/sample_queries/orders.sql  
      Content (single line, UTF-8, LF):  
          SELECT * FROM orders WHERE order_date > '2023-01-01';
   b) /home/user/sample_queries/inventory.sql  
      Content (single line, UTF-8, LF):  
          SELECT * FROM inventory WHERE quantity < 10;

2. No /home/user/db_opt_project/ directory exists yet.

Expected final state after successful completion:

A. Directory tree
/home/user/db_opt_project/  
├── backups/  
│   └── queries_backup_20240115.tar.gz  
├── queries/  
│   ├── orders.sql            (exact copy of the original)  
│   └── inventory.sql         (exact copy of the original)  
├── results/  
│   ├── orders_optimized.sql  
│   └── inventory_optimized.sql  
└── optimization_log.csv  

B. backups/queries_backup_20240115.tar.gz
   • Must be a valid gzip-compressed tar archive.  
   • When extracted, the archive root must contain exactly two files named orders.sql and inventory.sql whose contents are byte-for-byte identical to their counterparts in /home/user/sample_queries/.

C. results/orders_optimized.sql  
   3 lines, UTF-8, LF.  
   Line 1 begins with “-- Optimized”.  
   Line 2 contains both:  
        1) the substring “orders”  
        2) the substring “USE INDEX”  
        3) the substring “order_date > '2023-01-01'”  
   Line 3 begins with “-- End”.

D. results/inventory_optimized.sql  
   3 lines, UTF-8, LF.  
   Line 1 begins with “-- Optimized”.  
   Line 2 contains both:  
        1) the substring “inventory”  
        2) the substring “USE INDEX”  
        3) the substring “quantity < 10”  
   Line 3 begins with “-- End”.

E. /home/user/db_opt_project/optimization_log.csv  
   • Exactly 3 lines.  
   • Line 1 (header) is:  
        filename,lines_original,lines_optimized  
   • Line 2 is:  
        orders.sql,1,3  
   • Line 3 is:  
        inventory.sql,1,3  

Permissions: all newly created items must be readable by the current non-root user. Write permissions are not checked after creation.

Automated test procedure:
1. Confirm presence of /home/user/db_opt_project/ and its three sub-directories.  
2. Confirm the tar archive name and that its contents match the original files via sha256sum.  
3. Inspect orders_optimized.sql and inventory_optimized.sql for 3-line structure and required substrings.  
4. Parse optimization_log.csv and verify exact three-line file with correct header and counts.  
5. Cross-check that the numeric counts in optimization_log.csv match actual wc -l of the corresponding files.  
Any deviation fails the test.
